text: The biochemical characterization of Rsa canina L. fruits is an increasingly expanding subject in the scientific literature. The fruits of Rsa canina L. are well-known sources of ascorbic acid, phenolics and flavonoids, and are rapidly becoming increasingly well characterised for their antioxidant, antimutagenic and anticarcinogenic activities. The aim of the present study was to evaluate and quantify the vitamin C, total polyphenols, total flavonoids of Rsa canina L. fruits and their antioxidant activity.
